ErrorResponse.description:
Standardized error response with detailed information for troubleshooting.

This schema provides structured error information that enables:
1. Understanding what went wrong (error_code, message)
2. Knowing exactly how to fix it (fix_suggestion)
3. Identifying specific field issues (field_errors)
4. Finding additional help (documentation_url)

All error responses from this API follow this schema for consistency.

field_errors.message: Value must be a positive integer

example.fix_suggestion: Ensure all required fields are present and have valid values. See field_errors for details.

documentation_url.description: URL to relevant documentation for this error or endpoint
documentation_url.title: Documentation Url

error_code.description: Machine-readable error code in SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE format. Use this to programmatically identify error types. Common codes: VALIDATION_ERROR, INVALID_CREDENTIALS, RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND, RATE_LIMITED, INTERNAL_ERROR, CONFIGURATION_MISSING
error_code.title: Error Code

field_errors.description: For validation errors (422), detailed list of which fields failed and why
field_errors.title: Field Errors

fix_suggestion.description: Actionable instruction that explains exactly how to fix this error and retry the request successfully. Be specific about what to change.
fix_suggestion.example: Set 'numberOfDevices' to a positive integer (e.g., 100). This field represents the number of IoT devices in your deployment.
fix_suggestion.title: Fix Suggestion

http_status.description: The HTTP status code associated with this error (400, 401, 403, 404, 422, 500, etc.)
http_status.title: Http Status

message.description: Human-readable description of what went wrong
message.example: Request validation failed: numberOfDevices must be positive
message.title: Message

ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse

FieldError.description:
Describes a validation error for a specific request field.

Use this to understand exactly which field failed validation
and what values would be acceptable.

allowed_values.description: If applicable, the list of valid values for this field
allowed_values.title: Allowed Values

field.description: The name of the field that failed validation (dot notation for nested fields)
field.title: Field

message.description: Human-readable explanation of why this field failed validation
message.example: Value must be a positive integer
message.title: Message

received_value.description: The actual value that was received (stringified for display)
received_value.title: Received Value

FieldError.title: FieldError

get.description:
Check if redeployment is allowed (GCP Firestore 5-min cooldown).

**Zero cloud costs:** Pure calculation with hardcoded 5-min limit. No cloud API calls.

Args:
    destroyed_at: ISO timestamp from Management API
    uses_gcp_firestore: Whether deployment uses GCP as L3-Hot provider

Returns:
    ready: True if deployment can proceed
    remaining_seconds: Seconds until ready (0 if ready)

post.description:
Deploys the full digital twin environment using Terraform.

**Deployment process:**
1. Validates project structure and configuration
2. Runs `terraform init` (if needed) and `terraform apply`
3. Deploys all configured layers based on config_providers.json

**Layers deployed:**
- **L1** (IoT Ingestion): IoT Hub/Core, Dispatcher Lambda/Function
- **L2** (Processing): Persister, Event Checker, State Machine
- **L3** (Storage): Hot storage (DynamoDB/CosmosDB), Cold storage (S3/Blob)
- **L4** (Digital Twin): TwinMaker/ADT entities
- **L5** (Visualization): Grafana dashboards

**Note:** Long-running operation (2-10 minutes depending on resources).

parameters.description: Cloud provider: aws, azure, or google
parameters.name: provider

schema.description: Cloud provider: aws, azure, or google
schema.title: Provider

400.description: Invalid project or provider

500.description: Deployment failed

post.description:
Deploy with Server-Sent Events streaming.

Returns an SSE stream with real-time deployment logs.

parameters.name: provider

schema.description: Cloud provider: aws, azure, or google
schema.title: Provider

400.description: Invalid project or provider

500.description: Deployment failed

post.description:
Destroys the full digital twin environment using Terraform.

**Destruction process:**
1. Runs `terraform destroy` to remove all infrastructure
2. Cleans up SDK-managed resources (IoT devices, Digital Twin entities)

**If destruction fails for AWS TwinMaker:**
Use `DELETE /projects/{name}/cleanup/aws-twinmaker` to manually clean entities first.

**Note:** This operation cannot be undone. All data will be lost.

parameters.description: Cloud provider: aws, azure, or google
parameters.name: provider

schema.description: Cloud provider: aws, azure, or google
schema.title: Provider

500.description: Destruction failed - may need force cleanup

post.description:
Destroy with Server-Sent Events streaming.

Returns an SSE stream with real-time destruction logs.

schema.description: Cloud provider: aws, azure, or google
schema.title: Provider

500.description: Destruction failed

get.description:
**Purpose:** Unified status check for infrastructure, user functions, and SDK-managed resources.

**Returns three categories:**
- `infrastructure`: Terraform-managed resources (fast, local state check)
- `user_functions`: Function deployment status (from hash metadata)
- `sdk_managed`: Dynamic resources (TwinMaker entities, IoT devices)

**Performance:**
- Default: ~instant (local state file)
- With `detailed=true`: 5-30 seconds (cloud API drift detection)

get.description:
**Purpose:** Dashboard overview for Flutter frontend.

**When to call:** When displaying project cards or details.

**Returns:** name, description, providers, deployment/validation status.

example.fix_suggestion: Use GET /projects to list available projects, then use an existing project name

404.description: Not Found - The requested resource does not exist

get.description:
**Purpose:** Pre-deployment readiness check for existing projects.

**Checks performed:**
- Required files presence
- Config content validity (schema validation)
- Optimization flag dependencies
- Cross-config consistency (payloads ↔ devices, credentials ↔ providers)

parameters.description: Name of the project structure to validate
parameters.name: project_name

schema.description: Name of the project structure to validate
schema.title: Project Name

example.fix_suggestion: Include 'provider' parameter with value 'aws', 'azure', or 'gcp'

400.description: Bad Request - The request was malformed or missing required parameters

example.fix_suggestion: Use GET /projects to list available projects, then use an existing project name

404.description: Not Found - The requested resource does not exist

post.description:
**Purpose:** Validates a specific configuration file against its JSON schema.

**When to call:** To validate individual config files (config.json, config_iot_devices.json, etc).

**Path param:** config_type = config|iot|events|providers|optimization|credentials|aws_hierarchy|azure_hierarchy

example.fix_suggestion: Include 'provider' parameter with value 'aws', 'azure', or 'gcp'

400.description: Bad Request - The request was malformed or missing required parameters

example.fix_suggestion: Retry the request. If the error persists, check server logs or contact support.
example.message: An unexpected error occurred while processing your request

500.description: Internal Server Error - An unexpected error occurred

post.description:
Validates ALL deployer configuration files and functions for state transition to 'configured'.

**Checks performed (reuses existing validators):**
- `deployer_digital_twin_name`: Non-empty, valid format (alphanumeric, hyphen, underscore, max 15 chars)
- `config_events`, `config_iot_devices`: Required, schema validation
- `payloads`: Required
- `processors`: One per device in config_iot_devices, code validation
- `hierarchy`: Required if L4 = AWS or Azure, schema validation
- `scene_config`, `scene_glb`: Required if needs3DModel = true and L4 = AWS/Azure
- `event_feedback`: Required if returnFeedbackToDevice = true
- `event_actions`: One per action functionName in config_events (if useEventChecking = true)
- `state_machine`: Required if triggerNotificationWorkflow = true
- `user_config`: Required if L5 = AWS or Azure

**Returns all errors, not just the first one.**

**Purpose:** Validates Python code syntax and entry point signatures for serverless functions.

**When to call:** To validate processor or event action code before deployment.

**Checks:** Python syntax (ast.parse), required function signature per provider.

example.fix_suggestion: Include 'provider' parameter with value 'aws', 'azure', or 'gcp'

400.description: Bad Request - The request was malformed or missing required parameters

example.fix_suggestion: Retry the request. If the error persists, check server logs or contact support.
example.message: An unexpected error occurred while processing your request

500.description: Internal Server Error - An unexpected error occurred

post.description:
**Purpose:** Validates hierarchy JSON for L4 Digital Twins (AWS IoT TwinMaker or Azure ADT).

**When to call:** To validate aws_hierarchy.json or azure_hierarchy.json before deployment.

**AWS format:** Array of entity definitions with type, id, and optional children.
**Azure format:** Object with header, models, twins, and relationships arrays.

example.fix_suggestion: Include 'provider' parameter with value 'aws', 'azure', or 'gcp'

post.description:
**Purpose:** Validates that all iotDeviceId values in payloads.json exist in config_iot_devices.json.

**When to call:** To ensure payload device IDs match configured devices before simulation.

**Response:** Returns valid=true if all device IDs match, or list of mismatches.

post.description:
**Purpose:** Extracts and validates project zip for Flutter wizard auto-population.

**When to call:** From Step 3 wizard to import existing project files.

**Mode A:** Pass validation_context to skip credentials (wizard step 3).
**Mode B:** Full import with include_credentials=true.

parameters.description: Include credentials in response (Mode B only)
parameters.name: include_credentials

schema.description: Include credentials in response (Mode B only)
schema.title: Include Credentials

example.fix_suggestion: Include 'provider' parameter with value 'aws', 'azure', or 'gcp'

400.description: Bad Request - The request was malformed or missing required parameters

example.fix_suggestion: Check field_errors for specific issues and correct the values

422.description: Validation Error - Request body failed schema validation

example.fix_suggestion: Retry the request. If the error persists, check server logs or contact support.
example.message: An unexpected error occurred while processing your request

500.description: Internal Server Error - An unexpected error occurred

post.summary: Extract and validate project zip for wizard auto-population

tags.description: Project lifecycle management: upload, configure, validate, delete. Includes config retrieval, simulator download, and AWS TwinMaker cleanup.
